hotspots.details: Referenced by 5 classes (OwnerController, PetController, VetController, VisitController, PetTypeFormatter). Central service facade — all web tier routes through it. Any change here cascades across all controllers.
Largest file in main/java at 159 LOC. Generic abstract base for JDBC result set joining. Complex generic type logic (R, C, K). Used by JdbcPetVisitExtractor.
156 LOC, contains SQL join logic + lazy loading of pets and visits. Most complex repository implementation. Tight coupling between Owner, Pet, Visit via raw SQL and object mapping.
152 LOC. Aggregate root with cascade behavior. Any change to the pet ownership model impacts both JDBC and JPA layers. Contains bidirectional association management logic.
132 LOC, 7 HTTP endpoints. Busiest controller in the web layer. Handles both create/update flows. Relies on ClinicService.findOwnerByLastName which triggers a DB call per search.
111 LOC. Only concrete service implementation — single point of failure for all domain operations. Two @Cacheable methods (findPetTypes, findVets) — cache invalidation not explicitly managed.
121 LOC — surprisingly large for a simple entity. Contains JAXB/JPA annotations and pet-visit bidirectional association. Worth reviewing for simplification.
117 LOC. Unusual: this repository depends on OwnerRepository (constructor injection), creating a cross-repository dependency that can cause circular bean issues or coupling between aggregates.

note: No file exceeds 500 LOC — project has no monolithic files by threshold. Hotspots are coupling/reference-based, not size-based.
